title: Sri Lanka seeing first Yala paddy harvest from North and East: Minister
body_lines: ECONOMYNEXT – Sri Lanka is getting the first harvests from the Yala minor cropping season from the North and Eastern regions and farmgate paddy prices were around 120 rupees a kilogram, Agriculture Minister Mahinda Amaraweera said.
Due to fears of food shortages some people engaged in panic paddy buying driving the price of rough rice to around 170 or up to 200 a kilogram in some cases, he said.
However from an original estimate of 275,000 hectares farmers have now cultivated 475,000 hectares.
Sri Lanka has already imported 365,000 metric tonnes of rice he said.
